Now in this video what I want to show you is what the next decisions are Based on the data that we've got so when I look at this set of campaigns I'm looking for that stability as an indicator as to what to do next now number one if I've got good stable results on my Graduated campaigns on my ad sets then what I'm going to do is take them back to phase two of graduation testing Which is now bringing in new ads so for example if I go into my lookalikes campaign I have lookalike audiences there that are based on the different Pixel events so 1% of initiate checkout 1% of people have viewed a video view content at a car etc And what I can do at this stage is make some next decisions So if I've tested these 1% and they've gone through the graduation testing process So through phase one to see if the audience is good phase two to find ads additional ads at work But now I'm at phase three with these prospecting audiences. I know that they work so looking at this data I've spent for example one two three Four three three and a half K on these three ad sets and I've maintained a good row ass So that is far more confidence than I need but it's enough confidence to know that those three ads and you know This one's there or thereabouts as if your optimization tweaks still to be done But these three ad sets these audiences are working really well So what do I do next? I then push the 2% the 3% the 4% the 5% and Up to 10% into my test phase because the way I look at lookalikes is you start off with a core audience So for example, this is people that have initiated checkouts I believe in the last 90 days off the top of my head. So these are people that have initiated check out and I create the lookalike from that that lookalike data seems to work Now I want to take that back to phase two of my grad test because I know that the 1% works and test new ads and Then bait back to phase one of my grad test process and test the additional lookalikes So that there'll be a sheet on how to step through this in terms of the testing process But that's one of the first decision points I want to build in because this is this plate plays a big part into Optimization and growth because as you start to spend more money on your ads, especially for example This is 1% in the US a couple of million people if I now scale this from, you know, spending 1k a week to 10k a week to 100k a week I need bigger audiences and that 1% will exhaust fairly quickly But now if I've gone through the test process and I've introduced a 5% a 10% and they're also working with whichever ads are Resonating then I can build stability because as I scale up. I'm not worrying about Oh, you know, my audiences are starting to fatigue. So that's that's one of the decisions points The other decision point that I would be looking at is based on these particular 1% audiences and now one thing you'll notice here I have two ways of graduating my lookalikes Sometimes what I'll do is push them straight into what I call a super lookalike a super lookalike takes If similar sized audiences and groups them together So super lookalike would have taken these three Audiences which are the three the best audiences for me right now and group them into one ad set So that one ad set would have 1% initiate check out 1% of video views 95% 1% of view content in one ad set then what it would do is Duplicate that ad set two or three times and let facebook bounce between the ad sets in terms of spend But the reason we haven't done this for for right now is because when we went through Phase one of testing the actual audiences was slightly different So although these are all 1% audiences the age range and the demographic for who we were Targeting was slightly different. So although here we've got three 1% lookalike ad sets and and generally we would merge them into one We can't do that right now because of the difference in performance for the age range and also the gender as well So there's just something as a consideration. I always prefer fewer ad sets And and kind of super lookalikes and stacked interests for example So, you know, if I've got five different interests at work when I go to scale I'd rather combine those five But sometimes you do find that audiences work with different types of demographic and and that's really important because if you're If you're testing one audience a 1% lookalike 3% lookalike The seed audience which means the custom audience There could be a certain age range or gender that's in there which then Tells the lookalike what kind of other other people do look for But when you create the lookalike you might find different age ranges perform better So for example in this initiate checkout custom audience We targeted everyone that has initiated checkout But when we went through testing we found that the males 35 to 65 perform better than the females and that specific age range How did we do that because we went into our breakdown report in testing? And we looked at age and gender breakdown. So that gave us an idea of performance This also leads on to another point is now at this stage What what would I do to optimize and take that on?
